_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 6-2 Pediatric Symptomatic Bradycardia (Revised 12/30/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Pediatric Cardiac Protocol Section 6-2: Pediatric Symptomatic Bradycardia — MDHHS Approved 12/30/22, Reviewed 2023
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. Aliases: Slow heart rate, heart block. Bradycardia should be considered to be due to HYPOXIA until proven otherwise. This protocol applies to pediatric patients with bradycardia, a pulse, and poor perfusion (cardiopulmonary compromise). When CPR is required, a precise diagnosis of the specific bradyarrhythmia is not important.

sections.title: Signs of Cardiopulmonary Compromise
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 6-2: Pediatric Bradycardia — Compromise Criteria
points: HYPOTENSION: neonates SBP < 60; infants 1 month–1 year SBP < 70; children 2–10 years SBP < 70 + (age x 2); children > 10 years SBP < 90
SIGNS OF SHOCK: absent and/or weak peripheral and femoral pulses; capillary refill > 3 seconds; skin cool/mottled

RESPIRATORY DIFFICULTY: increased work of breathing (retractions, nasal flaring, grunting, tracheal tugging); cyanosis; altered level of consciousness (unusual irritability, lethargy, failure to respond to parents); stridor; wheezing

sections.title: General Treatment and Escalation
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable, utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Provide supplemental oxygen as needed to maintain SpO2 > 94%
If pulse < 60: confirm and support adequate oxygenation and ventilation
If pulse remains < 60 and patient remains symptomatic: perform CPR
Apply cardiac monitor to identify rhythm

If pulse remains < 60 despite oxygenation and ventilation: administer epinephrine per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC not available: 1 mg/10 mL; 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg) IV/IO up to 1 mg (10 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes

If patient remains unstable and pulse < 60: administer atropine per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC not available: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (minimum dose 0.1 mg; maximum single dose 0.5 mg); may repeat ONCE in 5 minutes if effective; continue epinephrine administration as above

If patient remains unstable and pulse < 60 after epinephrine AND atropine: begin transcutaneous pacing at rate up to 100 bpm per Electrical Therapy-Procedure Protocol; sedation may be used to facilitate TCP per MCA selection — refer to Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Continuously monitor for pulses — if pulse is not present: refer to Pediatric Cardiac Arrest-Treatment Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether sedation may be administered per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol to facilitate transcutaneous pacing in pediatric patients
options: Authorized per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine IV/IO (Pediatric Bradycardia)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 1 mg/10 mL — 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg) IV/IO; maximum dose 1 mg (10 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes
key_drugs.indication: Pulse < 60 with cardiopulmonary compromise despite adequate oxygenation and ventilation
key_drugs.contraindications: Use caution — consider hypoxia as the cause first; support oxygenation and ventilation before medications
key_drugs.notes: Bradycardia is due to HYPOXIA until proven otherwise — ensure adequate oxygenation and ventilation before and during epinephrine administration. Use MI-MEDIC for weight-based dosing.

key_drugs.name: Atropine IV/IO (Pediatric Bradycardia)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO — minimum 0.1 mg; maximum single dose 0.5 mg; may repeat ONCE in 5 minutes if effective
key_drugs.indication: Persistent symptomatic bradycardia (pulse < 60) after epinephrine and ongoing CPR if needed
key_drugs.contraindications: Minimum dose 0.1 mg — paradoxical bradycardia with sub-therapeutic doses; maximum 0.5 mg per dose
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. Minimum 0.1 mg per dose to prevent paradoxical bradycardia. Continue epinephrine concurrently.

deterioration_flags: Pulse < 60 with poor perfusion — HYPOXIA first; ensure oxygenation and ventilation; then epinephrine IV/IO
Pulse < 60 after epinephrine — add atropine IV/IO; continue epinephrine
Pulse < 60 after epinephrine AND atropine — transcutaneous pacing rate up to 100 bpm; sedation per MCA selection
Pulse absent — refer to Pediatric Cardiac Arrest-Treatment Protocol immediately
Signs of shock with bradycardia — shock may be CAUSING bradycardia via hypoxia; treat shock and airway simultaneously
scope_notes: This protocol is PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
Airway management, oxygen, monitoring: Paramedic — may delegate CPR to lower levels per cardiac arrest protocol if needed
Sedation for TCP: per MCA selection per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
